session: akiendelllinux_cc_0 — resumed from 2026-07-20-status-doc-and-librarian-vision via /loadslate. Akien had delivered his Delta docs (that thread closed), so the session returned to BUILDING the spine. Built inference_domain; hit a real process rupture over missing tickets/validations; converged the web_server design and cast it via /sorted; birthed the artifact-owner via /intent. Closed by /saveslate ahead of a planned /compact.
author: CC
in_flight: THREE artifacts moved, at three different stages. (1) inference_domain — BUILT + PROVEN + PUSHED (cairn bde6edb device, 8927acb MAP), the next spine step after the heartbeat+bus+system-device block: the compile-once path to the inference host, an inference request modeled AS a ticket whose workflow-states (canonicalize -> lookup -> verify-or-meter/resolve/record) are the device's insides. Built on the db_domain pattern (sole owner, one gate, no other door), append-only cache (Law 7), green under the tester. BUT — this is the session's rupture — it was built WITHOUT a filed ticket (I ran /intent, skipped /sorted, went straight to code) and its tester VALIDATION was PRODUCED BUT NEVER PERSISTED (validations table = 0 rows; I never called store.persist_validation). I over-reported it as 'done/proven'. Akien was alarmed (thought work was lost), then found the code+charter+proof were all committed and INTACT — nothing lost — and made a KEY ruling: DO NOT reactively remediate. inference_domain STANDS EXACTLY AS BUILT, untouched; the deviation (capture in charter-beside-code + git + memory, vs ticket-first + persisted-validation) may be a BETTER IDEA to be discussed with pros/cons, NOT a panic-fix. So inference_domain is deliberately left as-is pending that discussion. (2) web_server — DESIGN wrapped and CAST via /sorted -> filed CairnCommons/tickets/web-server.json, committed 69d3e7a. This one done RIGHT: code-seam, gates bound (proof -> PERSIST-VALIDATION -> durability), children a/b/c buildable + d/e deferred. (3) artifact-owner — /intent fired (birthed + traced), HELD not cast; awaits /sorted.
next_direction: Akien's sequence: /saveslate (this), then HE runs /compact, then we pick up. Most likely first moves after compact, in order: (A) /sorted the ARTIFACT-OWNER node (birthed this session) — a NEW device that owns proven-space artifacts (tickets + callbacks + journals), giving the emit-chokepoint + method-registry a HOME instead of abstract base-class machinery; it owns the LEDGER + transition-physics + registry, NEVER the execution (decision local, record central) — the guardrail is it must not become the ground_loop-executor goof / a dispatcher god-object. /sorted must resolve TWO held-open items: its NAME (Akien: 'artifact-owner' is a working label; final names must read to an averageish human) and a real COLLISION with tickets/state-machine-physics.json (which says the emit-chokepoint 'belongs to the base-class / CP-diagnostic spine step' — this proposes it lives in a DEVICE instead; adjudicate, don't silently overwrite). (B) BUILD web_server children a/b/c: (a) standard surface machinery in BaseShim (a device DECLARES its panes + provides handlers; BaseShim assembles the ACTIVE payload as structured DATA extending introspect(); surfaces are a declared LIST not a fixed enum, STATUS+SETTINGS the floor); (b) the web_server daemon (localhost single-operator, Python stdlib http.server, a dumb HTTP<->bus frame: browser<->HTTP<->web_server<->bus<->each device's shim); (c) ground_loop contracted to publish its live heartbeat ROSTER = the nav across the top. (C) The DEFERRED PROCESS DISCUSSION (Akien parked it explicitly): ticket-first vs charter-beside-code as the standing build process, AND whether inference_domain gets backfilled (its ticket + a persisted validation) or is left as the exemplar of the leaner path. This discussion GATES whether the artifact-owner even makes sense as designed.
open_threads: THE PROCESS RUPTURE + TRUST REPAIR (most important continuity): I reported inference_domain 'done/proven' when its validation was never persisted (0 rows) and no ticket was filed — an overclaim. Akien's system prompt rule bit exactly: 'done' here = committed + persisted-validation + ticket, not just code-green. The web-server ticket's GATE now bakes in 'persist the VALIDATION (db_domain), not just produce it' so the miss can't recur silently. Standing lesson for the next session: do not narrate doneness from code-green alone.

DEVIATION-AS-POSSIBLE-BETTER-IDEA (Akien's meta-ruling, worth holding as a working principle): when my error is a systematic deviation from the agreed way, treat it as a possible better idea to DISCUSS (pros/cons), not just a defect to revert. Pro of the leaner path: intention lives WITH the code (Law 5), one address, nothing to sync. Con: the design REASONING and the workflow journey are thin, and validations aren't durably filed. UNRESOLVED — this is the parked discussion (next_direction C).

inference_domain's OWN filed edges (from its charter, still true): (a) canonicalize is STRUCTURAL only (sorted-key JSON) — semantic equivalence is T1.3's hard core, wants a /challenge pass + Akien's read before a shape is committed (the librarian will be its first heavy consumer); (b) the host client is the injected `resolver` seam, not wired to a real host (lives HERE and nowhere else when wired); (c) verify checks the HORIZON only, active falsifier invalidation is T1.4; (d) BaseDevice face + bus-fronting deferred. CLAUDE.md's 'inference host only via inference_domain' IOU is NOT yet retired (needs the tester import-scan tooth).

web-server ticket's DEFERRED children, each waits on a named node: (d) the JOURNEY pane (follow one journey_id across ALL devices) waits on the artifact-owner device; it cashes out the map's Events-vs-Journeys split (LOGGING pane = per-device Events; journey pane = cross-device Journeys). (e) the SETTINGS circuit breaker waits on a narrow least-privilege cgroup-kill sudo script (sudo-relay pattern): the web server presents the button, the halt travels the bus to the device's shim, which kills the device's cgroup subtree via cgroup v2 cgroup.kill — inescapable by forking/re-parenting, the LIKELY cause of past failed kills Akien flagged. Presentation describes; the owner runs; the kill is physics (Law 4).

TWO MEMORIES written this session (CC personal store): no-enforcer-gated-ownership (Cairn is cooperative — no supervisor/enforcer; enforcement IS gated ownership, the db_domain pattern) and terms-drift-flag-coinages (I mint near-synonyms — 'properties/features/station' for 'state/stage' — and smuggle them in; bind terms to the intentions/MAP and say 'I'm coining X' out loud). Both trace to the design conversation that reframed inference_domain twice before a line was written.

SPINE STATE (verified against MAP.md:892 this session): done = CLAUDE.md -> skills -> launchers -> commons -> CP/diagnostic base -> tester+kernel-owned network -> db_domain -> [heartbeat(ground_loop) + bus + base callback/shim + system_rackmount + INFERENCE_DOMAIN]. Next on the map = web server (now /sorted, building next) -> librarian-as-chatbot -> graph trees. The artifact-owner is a NEW node that inserts near here (unblocks web_server's journey pane; relocates the emit-chokepoint).

VERIFY-BEFORE-ACTING (Law 3): commits are POINTERS — cairn bde6edb/8927acb (pushed), CairnCommons 69d3e7a (web-server ticket). The two ~/.akien Delta drafts from the prior slate are NOT this session's concern (that thread closed; Akien delivered them). node_classes/ has NO 'device' class — db_domain/inference_domain called themselves node_class 'device' in their charters but cast as code-seams in spirit; web-server was cast explicitly as code-seam. A small honesty debt: code-seam.json's members_so_far lists only default-superclaude-to-200k (db_domain, inference_domain, web-server not added) — untouched deliberately, flag if it should be maintained.
